_description: Rust workspace and systems programming anti-patterns — practitioner-informed rules from BurntSushi, matklad, dtolnay, Amos (fasterthanlime), and Mara Bos

rules.message: [architecture] Chained .clone() — consider borrowing or restructuring ownership
[debugging-residue] #[allow(dead_code)] — remove unused code instead of suppressing warnings
[debugging-residue] todo!/unimplemented!/FIXME/HACK — implement or remove before committing
[convention] Empty .expect("") message — provide a descriptive panic message explaining the invariant
[architecture] anyhow in library code — use thiserror for typed errors in library public APIs, reserve anyhow for applications
rules._source: David Tolnay / dtolnay (docs.rs/dtolnay), Andrew Gallant / BurntSushi (blog.burntsushi.net)

rules.message: [debugging-residue] dbg!() macro left in code — remove before committing
[error-boundary] panic!/unreachable! in non-test code — use Result and ? operator for recoverable errors
[debugging-residue] println!/eprintln! in library code — use tracing or log crate instead
